questions.q: To which dynasty did Mihira Bhoja belong?
questions.explanation: Mihira Bhoja was a ruler of the Gurjara-Pratihara dynasty of northern India.

questions.q: In which century did Mihira Bhoja primarily rule?
questions.explanation: Mihira Bhoja reigned during the 9th century CE.

questions.q: Which Hindu deity was Mihira Bhoja especially devoted to?
questions.explanation: Mihira Bhoja was a devotee of Vishnu, as reflected in his epithets and coinage.

questions.q: Control of which great city was the central prize contested during Mihira Bhoja's era?
questions.explanation: Kannauj was the coveted imperial capital sought by the major northern powers of the age.

questions.q: The Gurjara-Pratiharas ruled primarily over which part of India?
questions.explanation: The Gurjara-Pratiharas held a vast realm across northern India.

questions.q: By what shorter name is Mihira Bhoja also commonly known?
questions.explanation: He is often referred to simply as Bhoja I of the Pratihara dynasty.

questions.q: The three-way struggle of Mihira Bhoja's time is known to historians as the contest for what?
questions.explanation: The Tripartite Struggle was the long contest among three powers for control of Kannauj.

questions.q: Mihira Bhoja was one of the most powerful rulers of which broad period of Indian history?
questions.explanation: He flourished in the early medieval period, roughly the 9th century CE.

questions.q: What kind of figure was Mihira Bhoja within Indian history?
questions.explanation: Mihira Bhoja was a king who ruled a vast realm in northern India.

questions.q: The Gurjara-Pratiharas are remembered for resisting raids by which group from the northwest?
questions.explanation: The Pratiharas are credited with checking Arab expansion from Sindh into India.

questions.q: Mihira Bhoja was the son of which Pratihara predecessor?
questions.explanation: Mihira Bhoja succeeded his father Ramabhadra to the Pratihara throne.

questions.q: Which son succeeded Mihira Bhoja as the Pratihara ruler?
questions.explanation: Mihira Bhoja was succeeded by his capable son Mahendrapala I.

questions.q: Besides the Pratiharas, which two dynasties were the other contenders in the Tripartite Struggle for Kannauj?
questions.explanation: The Palas of Bengal and the Rashtrakutas of the Deccan were the Pratiharas' rivals for Kannauj.

questions.q: Which city served as the principal capital of the Gurjara-Pratiharas under Mihira Bhoja?
questions.explanation: Mihira Bhoja ruled from Kannauj, the great imperial seat of the northern plains.

questions.q: An Arab traveller and writer who described the wealth and cavalry of the Pratihara king was named what?
questions.explanation: The Arab merchant Sulaiman left an account praising the Pratihara king's army and security of roads.

questions.q: Mihira Bhoja adopted which devotional title connected to Vishnu?
questions.explanation: Bhoja took the title Adivaraha, referring to the boar (Varaha) avatar of Vishnu, seen on his coins.

questions.q: Arab accounts of the period refer to the Pratihara realm by which name?
questions.explanation: Arab writers called the Gurjara-Pratihara land 'Al-Juzr', a rendering of Gurjara.

questions.q: Which prominent Rashtrakuta king clashed with the Pratiharas during this contest for the north?
questions.explanation: Rashtrakuta rulers such as Govinda III intervened forcefully in the northern struggle against the Pratiharas.

questions.q: The Gwalior prashasti (eulogy) inscription is an important source about which dynasty?
questions.explanation: The Gwalior inscription provides a genealogy and praise of the Pratihara kings including Bhoja.

questions.q: Under Mihira Bhoja, the Pratihara empire is generally regarded as having reached what?
questions.explanation: Bhoja's long reign is considered the height of Gurjara-Pratihara power.

questions.q: Roughly how long is Mihira Bhoja traditionally held to have reigned?
questions.explanation: Bhoja's reign is usually dated to roughly the mid-9th century over some four to five decades.

questions.q: Mihira Bhoja's reign is generally placed within which approximate span of years?
questions.explanation: Scholars generally date Mihira Bhoja's reign to about 836 to 885 CE.

questions.q: The Daulatpura copper-plate, associated with Pratihara administration, was issued during the line of which king?
questions.explanation: The Daulatpura plate is among the records connected with Mihira Bhoja's Pratihara administration.

questions.q: The Pala ruler who was a major eastern rival of Mihira Bhoja in the struggle for the north was which of these?
questions.explanation: Devapala of the Pala dynasty was the powerful eastern contemporary contending with the Pratiharas.

questions.q: The boar-avatar imagery on Bhoja's silver coins is identified with which incarnation of Vishnu?
questions.explanation: The Adivaraha coins depict the Varaha (boar) avatar of Vishnu lifting the earth.

questions.q: Which feudatory Chandela ruler acknowledged Pratihara overlordship during or near Bhoja's era?
questions.explanation: Early Chandela chiefs such as Harsha Chandela were feudatories under Pratihara suzerainty in this period.

questions.q: The name 'Mihira' in Mihira Bhoja is linked etymologically to which concept?
questions.explanation: 'Mihira' derives from a Sanskrit-Persian root for the sun, an honorific element in his name.

questions.q: Mihira Bhoja suffered an early reverse against which Rashtrakuta king before recovering his power?
questions.explanation: Bhoja faced setbacks against the Rashtrakuta Amoghavarsha I early in his reign before consolidating power.

questions.q: The Pratihara dynasty's name 'Pratihara' is traditionally said to derive from a role meaning what?
questions.explanation: The Pratiharas traced their name to the role of pratihara, a door-keeper, linked to legendary service to Rama's brother Lakshmana.

questions.q: The strong, secure, well-supplied cavalry of the Pratihara state was specifically praised in the account of which foreign writer?
questions.explanation: Sulaiman, writing in the 9th century, singled out the Pratihara king's fine cavalry and the safety of his roads.
